title: Autonomous Codebase Migration
creator: Reported by launch coverage
proof: Long-running autonomous software task used as a launch example.
whyItMatters: Shows sustained planning, repository-scale edits, and execution over a long horizon.

title: Pokemon FireRed Vision Playthrough
creator: Reported by launch coverage
proof: Played through a game using visual input.
whyItMatters: A legible demo for multimodal agency: perception, state tracking, and repeated action.

title: Survey Analysis Tool
creator: Reported by launch coverage
proof: Built as a multi-hour autonomous academic workflow example.
whyItMatters: Turns raw qualitative data into an artifact people can use, not just a text answer.

title: One-Prompt Voyager Documentary
creator: Higgsfield AI
proof: X post says Claude Fable 5 + Higgsfield MCP made a full Voyager documentary from a single prompt, sourcing public-domain NASA/JPL footage, planning 8 visual beats, filling gaps with labeled AI-generated shots, synthesizing TTS, and exporting the final MP4.
whyItMatters: This is not just image generation. It is a multi-step media production pipeline: research, source selection, beat planning, gap filling, narration, and final video assembly.
windowRule: Substantially shared during active availability window

title: One-Click Playable Story Game
creator: Higgsfield AI
proof: Quoted X post says Fable 5 researches the idea and writes a playable game with a full story while Higgsfield MCP generates all assets.
whyItMatters: It combines planning, writing, asset generation, and executable game assembly into a single creator workflow. Good gallery fit because the result is concrete, visual, and easy to understand quickly.
windowRule: Substantially shared during active availability window

title: Browser CAD Editor That Designs 3D-Printable Models
creator: Greg Feingold
proof: X post says Claude Fable 5 designed a complete 3D-printable model inside a browser-based CAD editor, and that the CAD editor itself, including the built-in AI copilot, was also created by Fable 5.
whyItMatters: This is a layered artifact: Fable creates the tool, the tool embeds an AI copilot, and that copilot produces a physical-world 3D-printable object. It belongs high in the gallery because it crosses software, design, and fabrication.
windowRule: Substantially shared during active availability window

title: Few-Prompt Interactive Website
creator: Asaad Mahmood
proof: X post says the site was designed in Fable 5 with just a few prompts and includes a 22-second screen recording.
whyItMatters: A strong gallery item because it shows the everyday creator end of Fable: not a lab benchmark, but a polished website made quickly enough that the craft question becomes taste, selection, and iteration.
windowRule: Substantially shared during active availability window

title: DataEmpire: Web Analytics as a Civilization Game
creator: Marc Lou
proof: X post says Marc Lou asked Claude Fable 5 to build a game on top of his web analytics API: every visitor becomes a villager, traffic grows an empire, and an Age of Legends unlocks at 1M visitors.
whyItMatters: This is one of the clearest examples of Fable turning boring business telemetry into a playful product surface. It is not merely a toy game; it wraps a real analytics API and gives users a reason to revisit their dashboard.
windowRule: Substantially shared during active availability window

title: Spawn 5.0: Weeklong Game/Engine System Push
creator: jacob / spawn
proof: Creator's X post says Spawn 5.0 was built with Claude Fable 5 across 1,687 prompts and 102 sessions, including a from-scratch physics engine, clustered froxel lighting, realtime diffuse GI on WebGPU, million-particle GPU VFX, MMO-scale netcode, and an alpha native iOS app.
whyItMatters: Even with creator-claim caveats, this is a major example of Fable as an extended engineering collaborator. The impressive part is not a single demo but sustained system evolution across rendering, physics, networking, and mobile surfaces.
windowRule: Substantially shared during active availability window

title: HermesWorld Automated MMO Redesign Loop
creator: Eric / outsource_
proof: Creator's X post says over 1B Fable 5 tokens were spent on HermesWorld, with Fable redesigning the MMO in a fully automated loop: integrating 12 maps, spawning in-game, taking screenshots for QA, root-causing crashes from logs, and shipping fixes in the next build.
whyItMatters: This belongs in the gallery because it shows Fable being used less like a chatbot and more like a persistent game-development worker: run, inspect, diagnose, patch, and rebuild.
windowRule: Substantially shared during active availability window

title: One-Shot Horror Game Live Demo
creator: SuggestionMission516
proof: Reddit post in r/singularity titled "It's over. Claude Fable 5 one-shots horror game live" includes a 4:30 video player and was surfaced by the /last30days Reddit pass.
whyItMatters: A full-length public gameplay demo is more digestible than a claim. The gallery value is immediate: visitors can watch the artifact and understand why one-shot game generation caught attention.
windowRule: Substantially shared during active availability window

title: AI-Lab Monopoly With Multiplayer Share Codes
creator: Justine Moore
proof: X post says Fable 5 recreated Monopoly with AI labs and startups as properties, including game rules, money, turns, and share codes for multiplayer games. The post includes a 22-second embedded video.
whyItMatters: This is a compact but unusually complete game artifact: recognizable rules, custom theme, stateful turns, money mechanics, and shareable multiplayer sessions. It is also highly legible for gallery visitors because the premise lands immediately.
windowRule: Substantially shared during active availability window

title: Working V8 Engine Model in CAD
creator: Aaron Li
proof: X post says Claude Fable 5 made a fully working V8 engine model in under 10 minutes and includes a 9-second embedded CAD video. A follow-up links to the Adam Fusion extension.
whyItMatters: This is one of the clearest Fable-era examples of AI moving from code/text into spatial engineering. The artifact is specific, mechanical, and immediately inspectable, with unusually high engagement for a CAD demo.
windowRule: Substantially shared during active availability window

title: Boeing 747 Designed in Autodesk Fusion
creator: Zach Dive
proof: X post says one prompt through Adam/Fable designed an entire Boeing 747 in Autodesk Fusion and includes a 26-second embedded video.
whyItMatters: A strong visual example of CAD automation: even if the model is a demo rather than production engineering, it shows Fable being used inside professional creation software instead of only generating standalone webpages.
windowRule: Substantially shared during active availability window

title: Limbo-Style Side-Scroller Clone
creator: Chris
proof: X post says Claude Fable 5 at extra-high reasoning produced a Limbo clone and includes a 42-second embedded video.
whyItMatters: This broadens the gallery's game set beyond board games and horror demos into physics/platformer feel. The post is terse, but the public video plus date and engagement make it a useful browseable artifact.
windowRule: Substantially shared during active availability window

title: One-Shot City Block Simulator
creator: Bilawal Sidhu
proof: X post says Claude Fable created a city block simulator with multi-agent traffic, live detection boxes and tracks, plus a day-to-night cycle, and that it one-shotted the result. The post includes a 24-second embedded video.
whyItMatters: This is the cleanest example of the one-shot awe pattern: not a single UI screen, but a miniature world with agents, visual instrumentation, and environmental time. The impressive part is coherence across interacting systems.
windowRule: Substantially shared during active availability window

title: Minecraft-Like World From One Prompt
creator: Noah Wachnik
proof: X post says Claude Fable made a Minecraft-like experience from one prompt in 20 minutes and includes a 19-second embedded video.
whyItMatters: The claim is provocative, but the gallery value is specific: voxel worlds make gaps in generation obvious. A plausible Minecraft-like loop suggests Fable can hold terrain, motion, interaction, and game feel together long enough to make a coherent toy world.
windowRule: Substantially shared during active availability window

title: QDD Actuator With Collision Validation
creator: Jake Fitzgerald
proof: X post says Claude Fable 5 designed a QDD actuator, animated the gearbox, and inspected collisions as part of the validation loop in roughly 30 minutes using about 400K tokens.
whyItMatters: This is a first-principles artifact, not a vibe mockup. The important signal is the validation loop: model, animate, inspect collisions, and iterate against physical constraints.
windowRule: Substantially shared during active availability window

title: Interactive Islamic Pattern Visualizer
creator: Misbah Syed
proof: X post says Claude Fable 5 one-shotted a playable geometric Islamic patterns visualizer where every seed generates a new downloadable pattern. The post includes a 16-second embedded video.
whyItMatters: This is a good counterpoint to pure engineering demos: Fable's appeal was also about making complete, beautiful, usable toys. It has generation, interaction, and export in one small polished surface.
windowRule: Substantially shared during active availability window

title: Playable V16 Engine in Three.js
creator: echo.hive
proof: X post says the Fable 5 + Three.js demo is a playable, sliceable V16 engine driven by slider-crank geometry from a single 45-degree crankshaft.
whyItMatters: It shows the first-principles theme in a browseable form: a mechanical object whose believability depends on geometry, motion, and constraints, not just surface-level aesthetics.
windowRule: Substantially shared during active availability window
